weather_line: Fog settling in the hollows · briefcase still glowing · ducks suspected

stories.headline: Something Ancient Stirs Beneath the Gorge: Enchanted Highlands Report 'Unusual Rumblings' Ahead of the Week's First Trail
body: Scouts along the fog-wreathed ridgelines of the enchanted Kentucky Gorge — a place where rock arches hum with old magic and the hollows fill with mist before first light — have filed an alarming report with this paper: something is stirring beneath Slade's deepest trails, and it appears to be in a very good mood about it.

Witnesses describe a faint trembling in the trail mud, a low harmonic resonance rising through stone, and at least one enchanted tree bending conspicuously toward the main trailhead as if straining to see whether the Jeep has arrived yet. A Ministry of Magical Creatures observer dispatched to the scene described the phenomenon as 'anticipatory,' before requesting a transfer to a quieter posting.

Steve, who investigated personally and declared the front suspension 'ready for whatever it is,' added that the Jeep itself seemed alert Monday morning in a way he found both encouraging and slightly personal. Christine has reviewed the route, cross-referenced three topographical charts, and identified what she calls 'the optimal line.' Steve has already found the same line instinctively and said nothing. All is as it should be.

stories.headline: Briefcase Now Humming in a Recognisable Key; Level Six Vending Machine Begins Dispensing Items Nobody Ordered
stories.byline: By our Ministry of Magic Correspondent
body: The mysterious glowing briefcase — which cleared Level Four security on Sunday by the simple method of making everyone too nervous to stop it — has escalated its presence at the Ministry of Magic. As of Monday morning, it is humming in a pitch that musicologists have identified, tentatively, as middle D. The Level Six vending machine has responded by dispensing unsolicited Pumpkin Pasties, a single shoe (left-footed), and what appears to be a small card reading 'you'll want this later.'

Two senior Aurors submitted a joint memo requesting guidance. The memo was returned with a Ministry stamp reading 'not our department' and a small smudge that smells faintly of something extraordinary. The briefcase continues to glow. Warm gold. All attempts to determine its contents have been met with a polite but absolute sense that this is not the right moment.

stories.headline: Chocolate Frogs Left as Offering Have Vanished; New Duck Arrives Bearing a Hand-Drawn Map (It Is Very Small)
body: The four Chocolate Frogs Christine placed on the Jeep's dashboard as an offering to the Duck Bandit were gone by Sunday evening. In their place: a seventh charmed rubber duck, this one wearing a tiny travelling cloak and holding, in one wing, a map approximately the size of a Knut. The map depicts what appears to be a trail — possibly in the Gorge — but is drawn at a scale requiring magnification to read.

Steve borrowed Christine's Omnioculars and confirmed the map shows 'some kind of route' before the image resolved further into what might be the Bandit's mark: a small duck silhouette stamped in gold. He has found no prints, no tracking charms, and no explanation. The tyres, again, are inexplicably clean. Experts are baffled. Christine has placed a fresh Chocolate Frog on the dashboard as reciprocal diplomacy. The Prophet considers this the correct move.

stories.headline: Goblin Sub-Committee Convenes Emergency Monday Session; Christine's Diary Was Already Blocked Out
body: In what veteran observers are calling 'absolutely typical,' the Gringotts Sub-Committee for Interdepartmental Account Reconciliation — an entity whose name alone has caused three junior clerks to faint — convened an emergency session at seven o'clock Monday morning, citing 'an irregularity of the third magnitude' in a file dating to 1987.

Sources confirm that Christine was not only present but had anticipated the session, arrived with the 1987 file already annotated in three colors, and had pre-emptively resolved the irregularity before the Sub-Committee's chairman had finished pronouncing 'interdepartmental.' The meeting adjourned in fourteen minutes. One goblin was overheard describing this as 'almost offensive.' Another straightened his hat and said nothing, which is widely understood as the highest possible compliment.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Investigators searching for the missing Chocolate Frogs are advised to look elsewhere — Lucy has constructed an airtight alibi involving the back garden, a patch of sun, and three independent witnesses who are also Lucy.
Ellie: Ellie spent a productive Monday morning introducing herself to the front window, which she found friendly, good-looking, and refreshingly willing to listen.
Jersey: Jersey has identified the warmest lap in the house, secured it by 7:02 a.m., and filed no further reports.
founding_editor_note: I built those presses to run on Mondays same as any other day — and I put a level on every single one, so there's no excuse for crooked headlines. Get on with it. — D.

in_brief.sport: Gringotts Invitational Lifting League update postponed; the trophy case has been triple-checked and reinforced, just in case.
in_brief.forecast: Persistent mist through the hollow until mid-morning, clearing on the ridge by trail time; high probability of mud, low probability of explaining any of it.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit
storyline_updates.summary: The Chocolate Frog offering vanished overnight. A seventh duck appeared, wearing a travelling cloak and carrying a tiny hand-drawn map showing what may be a trail route in the Gorge, stamped with a small gold duck silhouette. Steve cannot explain it. Christine has left another Frog. The Bandit appears to be communicating — possibly issuing an invitation.

storyline_updates.title: The Briefcase Mystery
storyline_updates.summary: The briefcase is now humming audibly in middle D. The Level Six vending machine has begun dispensing unsolicited items including a small card reading 'you'll want this later.' Two Aurors have requested guidance; Ministry returned their memo stamped 'not our department.' Briefcase remains unopened, contents unknown, glow still warm gold.
